sections.paragraph:
ἀπωθέω (ὠθέω ‘push’) fut. ἀπ(ε)ώσομαι; 1 aor. ἀπ(ε)ωσάμην; pf. ἀπῶσμαι; aor. pass. ἀπεώθην all LXX (Hom. et al.; pap, e.g. PFay 124, 19; LXX; TestAsh 1:8; TestJos 2:5; Philo, Aet. M. 74; Joseph.) in our lit. only mid. ‘push’ someth. ‘away’ or ‘aside.’

① push aside lit., w. acc. (4 Km 4:27; Jos., Ant. 17, 91) ἀπώσατο αὐτόν Ac 7:27 (like POxy 1206, 10).

② reject, repudiate, fig. ext. of 1 (sc. Moses) Ac 7:39. τὶ something (Appian, Syr. 5 §21; Quint. Smyrn. 9, 96) the word of God (Jer 23:17) 13:46; a good conscience 1 Ti 1:19 (cp. Jos., Ant. 4, 123; perh. maritime imagery Straub 27). Esp. of God: repudiate the people Israel Ro 11:1f (Ps 93:14; cp. 94:4 v.l. and oft. in LXX); of humans gener. Dg 9:2. τινὰ ἀπό τινος (Ps 118:10) force someone out of someth. B 4:13.—DELG s.v. ὠθέω. M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PFay PFay = Fayûm Towns and Their Papyri—List 4

TestAsh TestAsh = Testament of Asher, s. Test12Patr—List 2

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

someth. someth. = something

acc. acc. = accusative

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

ext. ext. = extension

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

perh. perh. = perhaps

Straub Straub = WS., Die Bildersprache des Apostels Paulus—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

oft. oft. = often

gener. gener. = generally

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 126
